title: Reentrancy vulnerability in claimLEND function allows double extraction
reason: Non-distinct and low value: reward-drain root cause is already captured by F-014 (accrued rewards never decremented), and the reentrancy precondition depends on a malicious callback-capable LEND token.

title: Division by zero when borrowIndex is zero in cross-chain borrow handler
reason: No concrete reachable path shown in protocol flow: cross-chain borrow records are initialized/updated with current market borrowIndex, so zero-index division appears to require corrupted state outside normal execution.

title: Oracle price returns zero without revert, creating silent liquidity bypass
Cross-chain liquidation execute fails silently and sends incorrect failure message
reason: Covered by existing liquidation-path findings (F-007, F-018, F-019); candidate combines multiple already-reported issues without a distinct new root cause.

title: Cross-chain repay does not verify srcEid validity before processing
reason: Largely a duplicate/reframing of existing repayment-record selection ambiguity in F-012; incorrect `_srcEid` input naturally reverts and is not a separate exploit primitive.

title: No deadline checks on cross-chain operations allowing stale execution
reason: General design concern without distinct exploit path beyond already-captured stale-snapshot TOCTOU risk in F-002.
